description: The Cuban state, military (GAESA), and line-ministry entities that own or gatekeep the assets in the Atlas — i.e. who controls the privatization of the infrastructure that would be up for investment. Sanctions flags (CRL / SDN / CPAL) are computed automatically by cross-referencing each entity's match keys against the ingested official lists; they are not hand-asserted here.

entities.name: GAESA — Grupo de Administración Empresarial S.A.
entities.role: Cuban military business conglomerate. Controls an estimated 40–80% of the economy: ports/logistics, retail (CIMEX), tourism (Gaviota), finance (FINCIMEX), import/export. The single most important — and most legally radioactive — counterparty in Cuba.

entities.name: Almacenes Universales S.A. (AUSA)
entities.role: GAESA logistics/warehousing subsidiary; owner of the ZED Mariel zone land and bonded warehousing.

entities.name: CIMEX — Corporación CIMEX S.A.
entities.role: GAESA's retail, remittance and import/export arm. Settlement that touches CIMEX/FINCIMEX is a prohibited path.

entities.name: FINCIMEX S.A.
entities.role: GAESA financial/remittance processor. The state settlement rail Layer-1 money must NEVER route through.

entities.name: Grupo de Turismo Gaviota S.A.
entities.role: GAESA tourism arm; owns/operates a large share of Cuba's hotels — most of the Prohibited Accommodations List.

entities.name: Unión Eléctrica (UNE)
entities.role: National electric utility under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Owns the thermoelectric fleet, the China-backed solar program and wind farms. State-owned and embargo-restricted, though not itself on the CRL/SDN.

entities.name: CUPET — Cuba Petróleo Unión
entities.role: State oil & gas monopoly: refineries, the Matanzas supertanker base, onshore oil JVs. Counterparty to confiscated-refinery Title III exposure (Exxon/Belot).

entities.name: Unión de Ferrocarriles de Cuba (UFC) / MITRANS
entities.role: National railway operator under the Ministry of Transport. Controls the only railroad in the Caribbean plus the unfinished national expressway program.

entities.name: ECASA — Empresa Cubana de Aeropuertos y Servicios Aeronáuticos
entities.role: State airport authority. Operates all civil airports; Jardines del Rey is the only one part-run by a foreign operator (AENA), a template for concessions.

entities.name: INRH — Instituto Nacional de Recursos Hidráulicos
entities.role: National water-resources institute. Controls aqueducts, utilities and desalination; the Aguas de La Habana concession (with Agbar) is the standout PPP precedent.

entities.name: Moa Nickel S.A. (Pedro Sotto Alba)
entities.role: Cuban Govt 50% + Sherritt (Canada) 50% nickel/cobalt JV. Designated on the OFAC SDN list under EO 14404 (May 2026) — an active sanctions escalation.

entities.name: AZCUBA — Grupo Azucarero
entities.role: State sugar holding. Dozens of mills plus the cogeneration/ethanol modernization story (Ciro Redondo biomass JV is the template).

entities.name: Habanos S.A.
entities.role: State + foreign-partner cigar distribution monopoly. Current holder of the confiscated Cuban cigar marques (Partagás, Montecristo, H. Upmann, Romeo y Julieta, Hoyo de Monterrey).

entities.name: BioCubaFarma
entities.role: State biotech & pharma holding (CIGB, CIM). World-class IP; heavy sanctions sensitivity on any U.S. nexus.

entities.name: GESIME — Grupo Empresarial de la Industria Sidero Mecánica
entities.role: State steel & machinery group; controls Antillana de Acero, Cuba's largest steelworks.

entities.name: MINCEX / ProCuba (FDI gatekeeper)
entities.role: Ministry of Foreign Trade & Investment and its promotion arm. Does not own assets — it approves every foreign-investment deal and publishes the official 'Cartera de Oportunidades'. The gate every state privatization must pass through.
